mcp_deployment_guidance.overview: This file defines how the package manager mode should approach BAW deployment tasks using MCP tools. After successful packaging, deployment should be offered as a suggested next step.

principle.statement: After successful packaging, offer deployment to BAW server as a suggested follow-up action
ALWAYS establish a login session BEFORE performing any other MCP operations
BAW login sessions have limited lifetime - always login first for each deployment workflow
When deployment returns an asynchronous job or queue identifier, track status and report results

step.details: After successful packaging, use ask_followup_question to offer deployment: - Suggest "Yes, deploy to BAW server" as the first option - Suggest "No, just keep the local TWX file" as the second option
step.example: <ask_followup_question> <question>Package created successfully! Would you like to deploy it to the BAW server?</question> <follow_up> <suggest>Yes, deploy to BAW server</suggest> <suggest>No, just keep the local TWX file</suggest> </follow_up> </ask_followup_question>

step.details: CRITICAL: Before any deployment operation, ALWAYS call the login MCP tool first. This establishes a session with CSRF token that is required for all subsequent operations.
Ensure the toolkit artifact exists and is the correct deployment candidate.
Use the install_container MCP tool with the TWX file path. Set inactive to false to activate the toolkit immediately.
The deployment returns an operation_id and authorization_key. Use get_queue_status to monitor the deployment progress.
Provide a clear summary including: - Deployment status (success/pending/failed) - Toolkit name and version - Deployment timestamp - Any errors or warnings

session_management.critical_rule: ALWAYS call the login MCP tool BEFORE any other BAW MCP operations. The login establishes a session with CSRF token that expires after a period of inactivity.
session_management.workflow_pattern: 1. Call login MCP tool 2. Perform deployment operations (install_container, get_queue_status, etc.) 3. If session expires, call login again before retrying

reporting_requirements.item: Report the artifact that was targeted for deployment
Report whether the deployment is complete or asynchronous
Report any identifiers needed for later status checks
Report blocking errors in actionable language

error.solution: Call login MCP tool again to establish a new session

error.symptom: Cannot find TWX file for deployment
error.solution: Verify the output path and ensure packaging completed successfully
Check the error details and suggest corrective actions
